title.zh: 卷十七 馮岑賈列傳
title.en: Volume 17: Biographies of Feng, Cen, Jia
title.raw: 卷十七 馮岑賈列傳 Volume 17: Biographies of Feng, Cen, Jia

sentences.zh: 馮異字公孫，潁川父城人也。

translations.literal: Feng Yi, courtesy name Gongsun, was a man of Fucheng in Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi, styled Gongsun, came from Fucheng in Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 好讀書，能《左氏春秋》、《孫子兵法》。

translations.literal: He loved to read and was versed in the Zuo Commentary on the Spring and Autumn Annals and in Sunzi's Art of War.
translations.idiomatic: He loved books and had mastered the Zuo Tradition of the Spring and Autumn Annals and Sunzi's Art of War.

sentences.zh: 漢兵起，異以郡掾監五縣，與父城長苗萌共城守，為王莽拒漢。

translations.literal: When the Han forces rose, Yi, as a clerk of the commandery supervising five counties, together with Miao Meng, the magistrate of Fucheng, jointly defended the city walls and resisted the Han on behalf of Wang Mang.
translations.idiomatic: When the Han armies rose, Feng Yi, serving as a commandery clerk overseeing five counties, joined Fucheng's magistrate Miao Meng in holding the city for Wang Mang against the Han.

sentences.zh: 光武略地潁川，攻父城不下，屯兵巾車鄉。

translations.literal: Guangwu seized territory in Yingchuan, attacked Fucheng but could not take it, and encamped his troops at Jinche Township.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Guangwu campaigned through Yingchuan, laid siege to Fucheng without success, and quartered his army at Jinche Township.

sentences.zh: 異間出行屬縣，為漢兵所執。

translations.literal: Yi went out secretly on business in subordinate counties and was seized by Han troops.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi slipped out on patrol of the dependent counties and was captured by Han soldiers.

sentences.zh: 時異從兄孝及同郡丁綝、呂晏，並從光武，因共薦異，得召見。

translations.literal: At that time Yi's cousin Feng Xiao, together with Ding Lin and Lü Yan of the same commandery, all followed Guangwu; they jointly recommended Yi, and he was summoned for an audience.
translations.idiomatic: At the time his clansman Feng Xiao and fellow commandery men Ding Lin and Lü Yan were already with Guangwu; together they recommended Feng Yi, who was then granted an audience.

sentences.zh: 異曰：『異一夫之用，不足為強弱。

translations.literal: Yi said: 'Yi's use is that of a single man; it is not enough to decide strength or weakness.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi said, 'I am only one man; my strength alone cannot tip the balance either way.

sentences.zh: 有老母在城中，願歸據五城，以效功報德。

translations.literal: I have an old mother in the city; I wish to return and hold the five cities so as to repay merit with merit and virtue with virtue.
translations.idiomatic: My aged mother is still inside the walls. Allow me to go back and secure the five cities for you, so I may prove my worth and repay your kindness.'

sentences.zh: 』光武曰：『善。

translations.literal: Guangwu said: 'Good.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu replied, 'Very well.'

sentences.zh: 』異歸，謂苗萌曰：『今諸將皆壯士屈起，多暴橫，獨有劉將軍所到不虜掠。

translations.literal: Yi returned and said to Miao Meng: 'Now the various generals are all stalwarts who have risen in revolt; most are violent and overbearing; only General Liu, wherever he goes, does not plunder.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi returned and told Miao Meng, 'The other generals are rough men who seized their chance to rise; most are brutal and predatory. Only General Liu takes no spoil wherever he marches.'

sentences.zh: 觀其言語舉止，非庸人也，可以歸身。

translations.literal: Observing his speech and deportment, he is no ordinary man; one may attach oneself to him.
translations.idiomatic: Watch how he speaks and carries himself: he is no common soldier. We can pledge ourselves to him.'

sentences.zh: 』苗萌曰：『死生同命，敬從子計。

translations.literal: Miao Meng said: 'Life and death share one fate; I respectfully follow your plan.
translations.idiomatic: Miao Meng said, 'We share one fate in life or death; I will follow your counsel.'

sentences.zh: 』光武南還宛，更始諸將攻父城者前後十餘輩，異堅守不下；

translations.literal: When Guangwu returned south to Wan, the generals of Gengshi who attacked Fucheng numbered more than ten parties in succession; Yi held firm and would not yield;
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu withdrew south to Wan, wave after wave of Gengshi's generals assaulted Fucheng, yet Feng Yi held the defenses and never gave way.

sentences.zh: 及光武為司隸校尉，道經父城，異等即開門奉牛、酒迎。

translations.literal: when Guangwu became Colonel Director of Retainers and his route passed through Fucheng, Yi and the others immediately opened the gates and presented oxen and wine in welcome.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu was appointed Colonel Director of Retainers and his road led through Fucheng, Feng Yi and his companions opened the gates at once, offering cattle and wine in submission.

sentences.zh: 光武署異為主簿，苗萌為從事。

translations.literal: Guangwu appointed Yi as chief clerk and Miao Meng as retainer.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu named Feng Yi chief clerk and Miao Meng a retainer.

sentences.zh: 異因薦邑子銚期、叔壽、段建、左隆等，光武皆以為椽史，以至洛陽。

translations.literal: Yi thereupon recommended his townsman Yao Qi, Shushou, Duan Jian, Zuo Long, and others; Guangwu all appointed them clerks and scribes, and thus they reached Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi then recommended fellow townsmen Yao Qi, Shushou, Duan Jian, Zuo Long, and others, all of whom Guangwu took on as clerks and scribes until they arrived at Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 更始數欲遣光武徇河北，諸將皆以為不可。

translations.literal: Gengshi several times wished to send Guangwu to campaign north of the Yellow River; the generals all deemed it inadvisable.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi repeatedly wanted to dispatch Guangwu to secure the region north of the Yellow River; his generals unanimously opposed the idea.

sentences.zh: 是時，左丞相曹竟子詡為尚書，父子用事，異勸光武厚結納之。

translations.literal: At this time Cao Xu's son Cao Xu held the post of imperial secretary; father and son wielded power, and Yi urged Guangwu to cultivate ties with them generously.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the left chancellor Cao Jing's son Cao Xu was an imperial secretary; father and son dominated the court, and Feng Yi urged Guangwu to win them over with lavish attention.

sentences.zh: 及度河北，詡有力焉。

translations.literal: When he crossed north of the Yellow River, Xu had a part in the strength of that effort.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu crossed the Yellow River into the north, Cao Xu proved instrumental.

sentences.zh: 自伯升之敗，光武不敢顯其悲戚，每獨居，輒不禦酒肉，枕席有涕泣處。

translations.literal: From the defeat of Bosheng, Guangwu did not dare show his grief openly; whenever he dwelt alone, he would not touch wine or meat, and his pillow and mat bore traces of weeping.
translations.idiomatic: After his brother Bosheng's defeat, Guangwu dared not display his sorrow in public; whenever he was alone he abstained from wine and meat, and his pillow and mat were stained where he had wept.

sentences.zh: 異獨叩頭寬譬哀情。

translations.literal: Yi alone knocked his head in obeisance and comforted him with words of sympathy.
translations.idiomatic: Only Feng Yi kowtowed and spoke gently to ease his anguish.

sentences.zh: 光武止之曰：『卿勿妄言。

translations.literal: Guangwu stopped him, saying: 'Sir, do not speak rashly.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu cut him short: 'Do not say such things carelessly.'

sentences.zh: 』異復因間進說曰：『天下同苦王氏，思漢久矣。

translations.literal: Yi again took advantage of a moment to advance counsel, saying: 'Under heaven all alike suffer from the Wang clan; they have longed for Han for a long time.
translations.idiomatic: Seizing another private moment Feng Yi urged him, 'All the realm has groaned under the Wangs; the people have hungered for the house of Han for years.'

sentences.zh: 今更始諸將從橫暴虐，所至虜掠，百姓失望，無所依載。

translations.literal: Now the generals of Gengshi run riot and are cruel and oppressive; wherever they go they plunder; the common people are disappointed and have nothing to rely on.
translations.idiomatic: Today Gengshi's generals rampage in tyranny, looting every place they reach. The people have lost hope and have nowhere to turn.'

sentences.zh: 今公專命方面，施行恩德。

translations.literal: Now you hold sole command in your quarter and carry out kindness and virtue.
translations.idiomatic: You alone command this region and can show them mercy and grace.'

sentences.zh: 夫有桀、紂之亂，乃見湯、武之功；

translations.literal: When there is the disorder of Jie and Zhou, then the merit of Tang and Wu is seen;
translations.idiomatic: It takes the chaos of a Jie or a Zhou to reveal the achievement of a Tang or a Wu.'

sentences.zh: 人久饑渴，是為充飽。

translations.literal: when people have long been hungry and thirsty, this is the time to fill and satisfy them.
translations.idiomatic: When men have starved and thirsted too long, that is the moment to feed them full.'

sentences.zh: 宜急分遣官屬，徇行郡縣，理冤結，布惠澤。

translations.literal: You should urgently dispatch subordinate officials to tour the commanderies and counties, settle unjust bonds, and spread favor and bounty.
translations.idiomatic: Send your officers at once through the commanderies and counties to redress wrongs and spread your benevolence far and wide.'

sentences.zh: 』光武納之。

translations.literal: Guangwu accepted this.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu took his advice.

sentences.zh: 至邯鄲，遣異與銚期乘傳撫循屬縣，錄囚徒，存鰥寡，亡命自詣者除其罪，陰條二千石長吏同心及不附者上之。

translations.literal: When he reached Handan, he sent Yi and Yao Qi by post-horse relay to pacify and inspect the subordinate counties, review prisoners, care for widowers and widows, remit the crimes of fugitives who surrendered themselves, and secretly list the two-thousand-bushel senior officials who were of one mind and those who were not, reporting them upward.
translations.idiomatic: At Handan he ordered Feng Yi and Yao Qi to ride the post relays through the attached counties, review those in custody, succor the widowed and alone, pardon fugitives who gave themselves up, and secretly memorialize which chief administrators stood with him and which did not.

sentences.zh: 及王郎起，光武自薊東南馳，晨夜草舍，至饒陽無蔞亭。

translations.literal: When Wang Lang rose, Guangwu galloped southeast from Ji, lodging in the open morning and night, until he reached Wulou Pavilion in Raoyang.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Lang rebelled, Guangwu raced southeast from Ji, sleeping rough night and day, until he halted at Wulou Pavilion in Raoyang.

sentences.zh: 時天寒烈，眾皆饑疲，異上豆粥。

translations.literal: The weather was bitterly cold; the host were hungry and exhausted, and Yi presented gruel of beans.
translations.idiomatic: The cold was severe; his men were famished and spent, and Feng Yi brought him a bowl of bean porridge.

sentences.zh: 明旦，光武謂諸將曰：『昨得公孫豆粥，饑寒俱解』及至南宮，遇大風雨，光武引車入道傍空舍，異抱薪，鄧禹熱火，光武對竈燎衣。

translations.literal: The next morning Guangwu said to the generals: 'Yesterday I received Gongsun's bean gruel; hunger and cold were both relieved.' When he reached Nan Palace he encountered great wind and rain; Guangwu drew his carriage into an empty house beside the road; Yi gathered firewood, Deng Yu kindled the fire, and Guangwu dried his clothes before the stove.
translations.idiomatic: The next dawn Guangwu told his officers, 'Thanks to Gongsun's porridge yesterday, I shook off both hunger and cold.' Farther on, at Nan Palace, a violent storm struck; Guangwu drove into a roadside hut while Feng Yi gathered fuel, Deng Yu lit the fire, and Guangwu dried his clothes over the flames.

sentences.zh: 異復進麥飯菟肩。

translations.literal: Yi again presented wheat rice and hare shoulder.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi then served him millet rice and hare haunch.

sentences.zh: 因復度虖沱河至信都，使異別收河間兵。

translations.literal: He then crossed again the Hutuo River to Xindu and sent Yi separately to gather troops in Hejian.
translations.idiomatic: They crossed the Hutuo again to Xindu, where Guangwu detached Feng Yi to levy troops in Hejian.

sentences.zh: 還，拜偏將軍。

translations.literal: On his return he was appointed lieutenant general.
translations.idiomatic: On Feng Yi's return he was named lieutenant general.

sentences.zh: 從破王郎，封應侯。

translations.literal: He followed in defeating Wang Lang and was enfeoffed as marquis of Ying.
translations.idiomatic: He took part in the destruction of Wang Lang and received the marquisate of Ying.

sentences.zh: 異為人謙退不伐，行與諸將相逢，輒引車避道。

translations.literal: Yi was by nature modest and retiring and did not boast; when traveling he met the various generals in the road, he always drew his carriage aside to yield the way.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi was humble and never vaunted himself: whenever he met another general on the march he pulled his cart aside to let them pass.

sentences.zh: 進止皆有表識，軍中號為整齊。

translations.literal: In advance and halt he always had distinguishing marks; in the army he was called 'orderly.'
translations.idiomatic: His camps and marches were marked by clear signals, and the troops nicknamed him 'the orderly one.'

sentences.zh: 每所止舍，諸將並坐論功，異常獨屏樹下，軍中號曰『大樹將軍』。

translations.literal: Whenever they stopped to lodge, the generals all sat together discussing merit; Yi always alone withdrew under a tree; in the army he was called 'the great-tree general.'
translations.idiomatic: At every halt the other generals sat in a circle boasting of their deeds, while Feng Yi habitually slipped away to sit alone beneath a tree, so the soldiers dubbed him 'the general under the great tree.'

sentences.zh: 及破邯鄲，乃更部分諸將，各有配隸。

translations.literal: When Handan had been broken, he then reassigned the various generals, each with his allotted command.
translations.idiomatic: After Handan fell he reassigned the commanders, each to a fixed contingent.

sentences.zh: 軍士皆言願屬大樹將軍，光武以此多之。

translations.literal: The soldiers all said they wished to serve under the great-tree general; Guangwu for this reason thought highly of him.
translations.idiomatic: The rank and file clamored to serve under the general of the great tree, and Guangwu prized him all the more for it.

sentences.zh: 別擊破鐵脛於北平，又降匈奴於林闟頓王，因從平河北。

translations.literal: Separately he struck and defeated Tiejing at Beiping, also subdued the Xiongnu king of Yulintun at the forest pass, and thereby followed in pacifying the region north of the Yellow River.
translations.idiomatic: He independently crushed Tiejing at Beiping, forced the submission of the Xiongnu king at Yulintun, and followed Guangwu in the pacification of Hebei.

sentences.zh: 時，更始遣舞陰王李軼、廩丘王田立、大司馬朱鮪、白虎公陳僑將兵號三十萬，與河南太守武勃共守洛陽。

translations.literal: At that time Gengshi sent the Prince of Wuyin Li Yi, the Prince of Linqiu Tian Li, the grand marshal Zhu You, and the Duke of the White Tiger Chen Qiao with troops said to number three hundred thousand, together with Henan governor Wu Bo, jointly to hold Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi meanwhile dispatched the Prince of Wuyin Li Yi, the Prince of Linqiu Tian Li, Grand Marshal Zhu You, and the White Tiger Duke Chen Qiao with a host reckoned at three hundred thousand, alongside Henan governor Wu Bo, to defend Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 光武將北徇燕、趙，以魏郡、河內獨不逢兵，而城邑宗，倉廩實，乃拜寇恂為河內太守，異為孟津將軍，統二郡軍河上，與恂合執，以拒朱鮪等。

translations.literal: Guangwu was about to campaign north to Yan and Zhao; because Wei commandery and Henei alone had not encountered warfare, while cities were intact and granaries full, he appointed Kou Xun governor of Henei and Yi as general of Mengjin, commanding the two commanderies' troops on the river in cooperation with Xun, to resist Zhu You and the others.
translations.idiomatic: As Guangwu prepared to drive north into Yan and Zhao, he noted that Wei and Henei alone had been spared the sword, their towns intact and their granaries full; he therefore named Kou Xun governor of Henei and Feng Yi general of Mengjin to command both commanderies' forces along the river in concert with Kou Xun and bar Zhu You's advance.

sentences.zh: 異乃遺李軼書曰：『愚聞明鏡所以照形，往事所以知今。

translations.literal: Yi then sent Li Yi a letter, saying: 'I have heard that the bright mirror is used to reflect form, past affairs are used to know the present.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi wrote to Li Yi: 'They say a bright mirror shows your face and the past illumines the present.'

sentences.zh: 昔微子去殷而入周，項伯畔楚而歸漢。

translations.literal: In antiquity Weizi left Yin and entered Zhou; Xiang Bo turned from Chu and returned to Han.
translations.idiomatic: Weizi left Yin for Zhou; Xiang Bo deserted Chu for Han.'

sentences.zh: 周勃迎代王而黜少帝，霍光尊孝宣而廢昌邑。

translations.literal: Zhou Bo welcomed the king of Dai and deposed the young emperor; Huo Guang honored Emperor Xiao and deposed the king of Changyi.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo welcomed the King of Dai and cast down the boy emperor; Huo Guang raised Emperor Xuan and removed the king of Changyi.'

sentences.zh: 彼皆畏天知命，睹存亡之符，見廢興之事，故能成功於一時，垂業於萬世也。

translations.literal: They all feared Heaven and knew their mandate, saw the signs of survival and extinction and the facts of abolition and rise, and therefore were able to succeed in one generation and hand down enterprise for ten thousand generations.
translations.idiomatic: Each read Heaven's will, grasped the signs of rise and fall, and so won success in his own day and left a name for ages.'

sentences.zh: 茍令長安尚可扶助，延期歲月，疏不間親，遠不逾近，季文豈能居一隅哉？

translations.literal: If Chang'an could still be shored up and the years drawn out, then—as the saying runs—outsiders cannot come between kin, nor the distant overreach the near: how could Ji Wen hold a single corner?
translations.idiomatic: Were Chang'an still worth propping up, you might buy time—but strangers cannot part close blood, and those far off cannot outrank those at hand. How long could you, Ji Wen, cling to one small corner?'

sentences.zh: 今長安壞亂，赤眉臨郊，王侯構難。

translations.literal: Now Chang'an is ruined and in disorder; the Red Eyebrows press on the suburbs; the princes stir up strife among themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Chang'an is a shambles; the Red Eyebrows ring the capital; the royal dukes tear one another apart.'

sentences.zh: 大臣乖離，綱紀已絕，四方分崩，異姓並起，是故蕭王跋涉霜雪，經營河北。

translations.literal: Great ministers are at odds; the bonds of rule are already severed; the four quarters split apart; different surnames rise together; therefore the Prince of Xiao toils through frost and snow and manages affairs north of the Yellow River.
translations.idiomatic: The great ministers are split; the state's fabric is torn; the realm fragments and rival houses spring up. That is why the Prince of Xiao braves ice and snow to build his position in the north.'

sentences.zh: 方今英俟雲集，百姓風靡，雖邠歧慕周，不足以喻。

translations.literal: At present heroes gather like clouds; the hundred surnames turn like wind and grass; though Bin and Qi admired Zhou, it is not enough as a comparison.
translations.idiomatic: Heroes flock to him like clouds; the people drift to him like grass in the wind—more readily than Bin and Qi once turned to the house of Zhou.'

sentences.zh: 季文誠能覺悟成敗，亟定大計，論功古人，轉禍為福，在此時矣。

translations.literal: If Ji Wen can truly awaken to success and failure, urgently settle the great plan, weigh his merit against the ancients, and turn calamity into fortune, the time is now.
translations.idiomatic: If you can read the tide of fortune, seize the great decision now, and match yourself to the worthies of old, you may yet trade ruin for glory—this is the hour.'

sentences.zh: 如猛將長驅，嚴兵圍城，雖有悔恨，亦無及已。』

translations.literal: If fierce generals drive straight on and stern troops besiege the city, though there be regret and remorse, it will also be too late.'
translations.idiomatic: But if crack troops sweep in and iron rings close around the walls, remorse will come too late to save you.'

sentences.zh: 初，軼與光武首結謀約，加相親愛，及更始立，反共陷伯升。

translations.literal: At first Yi and Guangwu had first formed a secret plot and were moreover intimately friendly; when Gengshi was established, they together brought about the ruin of Bosheng.
translations.idiomatic: Li Yi and Guangwu had once sworn a private pact and were close as brothers—yet when Gengshi took the throne they conspired to destroy Bosheng.

sentences.zh: 雖知長安已危，欲降又不自安。

translations.literal: Though he knew Chang'an was already in peril, he wished to surrender yet was not at ease in himself.
translations.idiomatic: Li Yi knew Chang'an was doomed yet hesitated to come over.

sentences.zh: 乃報異書曰：『軼本與蕭王首謀造漢，結死生之約，同榮枯之計。

translations.literal: He therefore replied to Yi's letter, saying: 'Yi originally with the Prince of Xiao first plotted to make Han, knotted a covenant of life and death, and shared a plan of glory and withering.
translations.idiomatic: He answered Feng Yi: 'I and the Prince of Xiao first plotted to restore Han; we pledged our lives together and swore to share honor or ruin.'

sentences.zh: 今軼守洛陽，將軍鎮孟津，俱據機軸，千載一會，思成斷金。

translations.literal: Now Yi holds Luoyang and the general guards Mengjin; both grasp the pivot; a meeting once in a thousand ages—think of forging metal firm as broken steel.
translations.idiomatic: You hold Mengjin while I hold Luoyang; together we grip the empire's hinge—an alliance forged once in a thousand years, strong as tempered steel.'

sentences.zh: 惟深達蕭王，願進愚策，以佐國安人。

translations.literal: Only penetrate deeply to the Prince of Xiao; I wish to advance a foolish plan to aid the state and settle the people.
translations.idiomatic: Please convey my utmost loyalty to the Prince of Xiao; I would offer my poor counsel to steady the realm and comfort the people.'

sentences.zh: 』軼自通書之後，不復與異爭鋒，故異因此得北攻天井關，拔上黨兩城，又南下河南成臯已東十三縣，及諸屯集，皆平之，降者十餘萬。

translations.literal: After Li Yi had sent his letter through, he no longer crossed swords with Feng Yi; therefore Feng Yi for this reason was able to attack north and seize Tianjing Pass, take two cities in Shangdang, then go south into Henan and pacify thirteen counties east of Chenggao as well as the various garrison settlements; all were pacified, and those who surrendered numbered more than one hundred thousand.
translations.idiomatic: From the day that letter went out, Li Yi ceased to contest Feng Yi in the field, so Feng Yi drove north, seized Tianjing Pass and two cities in Shangdang, then swept south of the Yellow River through thirteen counties east of Chenggao and every fortified camp, reducing them all and bringing over a hundred thousand men to surrender.

sentences.zh: 武勃將萬餘人攻諸畔者，異引軍度河，與勃戰於士鄉下，大破斬勃，獲首五千餘級，軼又閉門不救。

translations.literal: Wu Bo led more than ten thousand men to attack those who had turned aside; Feng Yi led his army across the river and fought Bo at Shixia, greatly defeated him and beheaded Bo, taking more than five thousand heads; Li Yi again closed the gates and did not come to the rescue.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Bo marched ten thousand soldiers against the defectors; Feng Yi crossed the river, met him at Shixia, shattered his army, and struck off his head with five thousand counted dead—while Li Yi kept his gates shut and sent no aid.

sentences.zh: 異見其信效，具以奏聞。

translations.literal: Yi saw his good faith and effect, and reported the whole matter in a memorial.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi saw how faithfully Li Yi had kept his word and laid the whole matter before the throne.

sentences.zh: 光武故宣露軼書，令朱鮪知之。

translations.literal: Guangwu deliberately published Yi's letter and let Zhu You know of it.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu deliberately leaked Li Yi's letter so that Zhu You would learn of it.

sentences.zh: 鮪怒，遂使人刺殺軼。

translations.literal: Zhu You was angry and thereupon sent men to assassinate Li Yi.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu You, enraged, had Li Yi murdered.

sentences.zh: 由是城中乖離，多有降者。

translations.literal: From this the city was divided within and many were those who surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Luoyang's defenders then turned on one another, and ever more men came over to the Han.

sentences.zh: 鮪乃遣討難將軍蘇茂將數萬人攻溫，鮪自將數萬人攻平陰以綴異。

translations.literal: You then sent the general against difficulties Su Mao with several tens of thousands to attack Wen, while You himself led several tens of thousands to attack Pingyin in order to tie down Yi.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu You dispatched Su Mao the 'general who settles strife' with tens of thousands against Wen while he himself led another host against Pingyin to pin Feng Yi in place.

sentences.zh: 異遣校尉護軍將兵，與寇恂合擊茂，破之。

translations.literal: Yi sent the colonel protector of the army with troops to join Kou Xun in a joint strike on Mao and defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi sent the colonel and protector of the army to unite with Kou Xun and crush Su Mao.

sentences.zh: 異因度河擊鮪，鮪走；

translations.literal: Yi thereupon crossed the river and struck You; You fled;
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi then crossed the river against Zhu You, who broke and ran.

sentences.zh: 異追至洛陽，環城一匝而歸。

translations.literal: Yi pursued to Luoyang, circled the city one full circuit, and returned.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi chased him to Luoyang, rode a complete circuit of the walls, and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 移檄上伏，諸將皆入賀，並勸光武即帝位。

translations.literal: When he forwarded his proclamation upward, the generals all entered to congratulate him and together urged Guangwu to assume the imperial title.
translations.idiomatic: Once his bulletin had gone up the chain of command, every general came to offer congratulations and pressed Guangwu to take the throne.

sentences.zh: 光武乃召異詣鄗，問四方動靜。

translations.literal: Guangwu thereupon summoned Yi to Gao and asked him about the movements in the four quarters.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu summoned Feng Yi to Gao and questioned him on conditions throughout the realm.

sentences.zh: 異曰：『三王反畔，更始敗亡，天下無主，宗廟之憂，在於大王。

translations.literal: Yi said: 'The three kings have rebelled; Gengshi is defeated and gone; under heaven there is no lord; the anxiety of the ancestral temples lies with the great king.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi said, 'The three kings have turned traitor, Gengshi is ruined, and the empire has no master. The fate of the house of Liu rests with you.'

sentences.zh: 宜從眾議，上為社稷，下為百姓。

translations.literal: You should follow the multitude's counsel—above for the altars of soil and grain, below for the hundred surnames.
translations.idiomatic: Accept what your officers urge—for the altars above and for the people below.'

sentences.zh: 』光武曰：『我昨夜夢乘赤龍上天，覺悟，心中動悸』異因下席再拜賀曰：『此天命發於精神。

translations.literal: Guangwu said: 'Last night I dreamed I rode a red dragon up to heaven; waking, my heart was moved with palpitation.' Yi thereupon left his mat and bowed twice in congratulation, saying: 'This is Heaven's mandate issuing from the spirit.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu said, 'I dreamed last night that I rode a scarlet dragon into the sky; I woke with my heart pounding.' Feng Yi left his seat, bowed twice, and said, 'That is Heaven proclaiming its charge within your inmost mind.'

sentences.zh: 心中動悸，大王重慎之性也。

translations.literal: The heart's palpitation is the great king's nature of grave caution.
translations.idiomatic: The pounding in your breast only shows the depth of your caution, my lord.'

sentences.zh: 』異遂與諸將軍議上尊號。

translations.literal: Yi then with the various generals deliberated on presenting the exalted title.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi then joined the generals in planning the bestowal of the imperial style.

sentences.zh: 建武二年春，定封異陽夏侯。

translations.literal: In the spring of the second year of Jianwu, his fief was fixed and Yi was made marquis of Yangxia.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of Jianwu 2 his enfeoffment was settled: Feng Yi became marquis of Yangxia.

sentences.zh: 引擊陽翟賊嚴終、趙根，破之。

translations.literal: He led an attack on the bandits Yan Zhong and Zhao Gen at Yangzhai and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: He led troops against the rebels Yan Zhong and Zhao Gen at Yangzhai and broke them.

sentences.zh: 詔異歸家上冢，使太中大夫賫牛、酒，令二百里內太守、都尉已下及宗族會焉。

translations.literal: He ordered Yi to return home and tend the ancestral tombs, sending a grand counsellor of the palace with oxen and wine, and ordering the governors, commandants, and those below within two hundred li together with clansmen to assemble.
translations.idiomatic: He directed Feng Yi to visit his home and sweep the family graves, sending a palace grandee with oxen and wine and commanding every governor and commandant within two hundred li, down to the lowest rank, and all kinsmen to gather for the occasion.

sentences.zh: 時，赤眉、延岑暴亂三輔，郡縣大姓各擁兵眾，大司徒鄧禹不能定，乃遣異代禹討之。

translations.literal: At that time the Red Eyebrows and Yan Cen ravaged the three approaches; great clans in the commanderies and counties each held troops in hosts; the grand minister over the masses Deng Yu could not settle them, and he therefore sent Yi in Yu's place to punish them.
translations.idiomatic: While the Red Eyebrows and Yan Cen terrorized the capital region and powerful local houses raised private armies, Grand Minister of Education Deng Yu could not restore order, so the court replaced him with Feng Yi.

sentences.zh: 車駕送至河南，賜以乘輿七尺具劍。

translations.literal: The imperial carriage saw him off to Henan and bestowed on him a seven-foot sword from the imperial equipage.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor escorted him to Henan and gave him a seven-foot sword from the imperial train.

sentences.zh: 敕異曰：『三輔遭王莽、更始之亂，重以赤眉、延岑之酷，元元塗炭，無所依訴。

translations.literal: He instructed Yi, saying: 'The three approaches have suffered the disorders of Wang Mang and Gengshi, compounded by the cruelty of the Red Eyebrows and Yan Cen; the common people are in mud and charcoal and have nowhere to appeal.
translations.idiomatic: His charge read: 'The three approaches have been wrecked by Wang Mang and Gengshi, then scourged by the Red Eyebrows and Yan Cen. The people lie in ashes and have no one to whom they may cry.'

sentences.zh: 今之征伐，非必略地屠城，要在平定安集之耳。

translations.literal: Now the purpose of this campaign is not necessarily to seize territory and slaughter cities; the essential point is to pacify and settle them.
translations.idiomatic: This expedition is not meant to grab land or sack cities; you are to pacify and gather the people in.'

sentences.zh: 諸將非不健鬥，然好虜掠。

translations.literal: The generals are not unfit for battle, yet they love to plunder.
translations.idiomatic: My generals fight well enough, but they cannot resist plunder.'

sentences.zh: 卿本能禦吏士，念自修敕，無為郡縣所苦。

translations.literal: You by nature are able to restrain officers and soldiers; think to discipline yourself and do not cause suffering to the commanderies and counties.
translations.idiomatic: You have always kept your men in hand—continue to hold yourself and them to strict discipline and spare the counties further pain.'

sentences.zh: 』異頓首受命，引而西，所至皆布威信。

translations.literal: Yi knocked his head and received the command, led his force westward, and wherever he arrived he spread authority and good faith.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi kowtowed, took the order, marched west, and everywhere his prestige and good faith went before him.

sentences.zh: 弘農群盜稱將軍者十餘輩，皆率眾降異。

translations.literal: In Hongnong the bandit chiefs who called themselves generals were more than ten parties; all led their hosts and submitted to Yi.
translations.idiomatic: More than a dozen rebel bands in Hongnong who styled themselves generals brought their followers to surrender to him.

sentences.zh: 異與赤眉遇於華陰，相拒六十餘日，戰數十合，降其將劉始、王宣等五千餘人。

translations.literal: Yi met the Red Eyebrows at Huayin; they opposed each other more than sixty days, fought several tens of engagements, and subdued their generals Liu Shi, Wang Xuan, and others, more than five thousand men.
translations.idiomatic: He met the Red Eyebrows at Huayin and for over sixty days they glared at each other across dozens of clashes until he won over their officers Liu Shi, Wang Xuan, and more than five thousand soldiers.

sentences.zh: 三年春，遣使者即拜異為征西大將軍。

translations.literal: In the spring of the third year an envoy was sent to appoint Yi on the spot as grand general on the west.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year an imperial messenger invested him on the field as grand general of the western expedition.

sentences.zh: 會鄧禹率車騎將軍鄧弘等引歸，與異相遇，禹、弘要異共攻赤眉。

translations.literal: It happened that Deng Yu led the general of chariots and cavalry Deng Hong and others in withdrawal and met Yi; Yu and Hong invited Yi jointly to attack the Red Eyebrows.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu was retreating with General of Chariots and Cavalry Deng Hong and the rest when he met Feng Yi; Deng Yu and Deng Hong pressed him to join them in striking the Red Eyebrows.

sentences.zh: 異曰：『異與賊相拒且數十日，雖屢獲雄將，餘眾尚多，可稍以恩信傾誘，難卒用兵破也。

translations.literal: Yi said: 'Yi and the bandits have opposed each other already several tens of days; though repeatedly capturing their brave generals, the remaining host is still many; they can gradually be overthrown with favor and good faith and lured; it is difficult suddenly to use troops to break them.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi objected: 'We have faced these bandits for weeks. I have taken some of their best commanders, but their numbers remain huge. They can be worn down with kindness and trust, not smashed overnight by force.'

sentences.zh: 上今使諸將屯黽池要其東，而異擊其西，一舉取之，此萬成計也。

translations.literal: The emperor now orders the generals to encamp at Mianchi to block their east while Yi strikes their west; to take them in one stroke—this is a plan of ten thousand successes.
translations.idiomatic: His Majesty wants other columns to hold Mianchi and seal their eastern flank while I hit them from the west—that is the sure plan to finish them in one blow.'

sentences.zh: 』禹、弘不從。

translations.literal: Yu and Hong did not follow.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu and Deng Hong would not listen.

sentences.zh: 弘遂大戰移日，赤眉陽敗，棄輜重走。

translations.literal: Hong thereupon fought a great battle lasting the whole day; the Red Eyebrows feigned defeat, abandoned baggage trains, and fled.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Hong attacked at once and fought all day; the Red Eyebrows pretended to flee, abandoning their wagons.

sentences.zh: 車皆載土，以豆覆其上，兵士饑，爭取之。

translations.literal: The carts all carried earth with beans spread on top; the soldiers were hungry and scrambled to seize them.
translations.idiomatic: The carts were loaded with sand topped by a thin layer of beans; the starving soldiers fell on the bait.

sentences.zh: 赤眉引還擊弘，弘軍潰亂。

translations.literal: The Red Eyebrows drew back and struck Hong; Hong's army broke in disorder.
translations.idiomatic: The Red Eyebrows wheeled and fell on Deng Hong, shattering his ranks.

sentences.zh: 異與禹合兵救之，赤眉小卻。

translations.literal: Yi joined forces with Yu to rescue them; the Red Eyebrows drew back a little.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi and Deng Yu rushed in together and checked the rout briefly.

sentences.zh: 異以士卒饑倦，可且休，禹不聽，復戰，大為所敗，死傷者三千餘人。

translations.literal: Yi considered that the soldiers were hungry and weary and could for the moment rest; Yu did not listen and fought again, and was greatly defeated by them, with more than three thousand dead and wounded.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi saw his men were famished and exhausted and begged for a pause; Deng Yu refused and attacked again, suffering a crushing defeat with over three thousand casualties.

sentences.zh: 禹得脫歸宜陽。

translations.literal: Yu obtained release and returned to Yiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu barely escaped and limped back to Yiyang.

sentences.zh: 異棄馬步走上回谿阪，與麾下數人歸營。

translations.literal: Yi abandoned his horse and went on foot up the Huixi Slope and with several men under his command returned to camp.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi left his horse and fled on foot up the slope at Huixi, reaching camp with only a handful of followers.

sentences.zh: 復堅壁，收其散卒，招集諸營保數萬人，與賊約期會戰。

translations.literal: Again he strengthened the walls, gathered his scattered soldiers, summoned and assembled the garrison camps to the number of several tens of thousands, and with the bandits agreed a day for a joined battle.
translations.idiomatic: He dug in, rallied stragglers, and drew several tens of thousands from the outlying stockades, then fixed a day to meet the enemy in the field.

sentences.zh: 使壯士變服與赤眉同，伏於道側。

translations.literal: He had stalwart men change clothes to match the Red Eyebrows and lie in ambush beside the road.
translations.idiomatic: He dressed picked warriors in Red Eyebrow garb and hid them along the route.

sentences.zh: 旦日，赤眉使萬人攻異前部，異裁出兵以救之。

translations.literal: At dawn the Red Eyebrows sent ten thousand men to attack Yi's vanguard; Yi barely sent out troops to rescue them.
translations.idiomatic: At daybreak the Red Eyebrows threw ten thousand men against Feng Yi's front; Feng Yi feigned a thin relief column.

sentences.zh: 賊見勢弱，遂悉眾攻異，異乃縱兵大戰。

translations.literal: The bandits saw the situation was weak; thereupon with their whole host they attacked Yi, and Yi then unleashed his troops in a great battle.
translations.idiomatic: Seeing weakness, they threw their entire force at Feng Yi, who then sprung his full strength in reply.

sentences.zh: 日昃，賊氣衰，伏兵卒起，衣服相亂，赤眉不復識別，眾遂驚潰。

translations.literal: When the sun was past the zenith the bandits' fighting spirit waned; the ambush troops suddenly rose, clothing was mixed together, and the Red Eyebrows could no longer tell friend from foe; the host thereupon panicked and broke.
translations.idiomatic: Past noon their ardor faded; Feng Yi's hidden men rose at once, and in the confusion of identical dress the Red Eyebrows could not tell ranks apart and collapsed in rout.

sentences.zh: 追擊，大破於崤底，降男女八萬人。

translations.literal: They pursued and struck, greatly defeated them at Yaodi, and subdued men and women to the number of eighty thousand.
translations.idiomatic: He pursued them, shattered their host at Yaodi, and accepted the surrender of eighty thousand men, women, and children.

sentences.zh: 餘眾尚十餘萬，東走宜陽降。

translations.literal: The remaining host still exceeded one hundred thousand; they fled east to Yiyang and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: More than a hundred thousand survivors fled east to Yiyang and gave themselves up.

sentences.zh: 璽書勞異曰：『赤眉破平，士吏勞苦，始雖垂翅回谿，終能奮翼黽池，可謂失之東隅，收之桑榆。

translations.literal: An imperial edict with the imperial seal praised Yi, saying: 'The Red Eyebrows have been broken and pacified; officers and men have toiled and suffered; at first though the wings hung down at Huixi, in the end they could flap their wings at Mianchi—one may say, lost in the east corner, recovered in the mulberry and elm.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's sealed rescript told Feng Yi: 'The Red Eyebrows are crushed. Your officers have borne a bitter campaign: first you seemed a clipped bird over Huixi, yet you spread your wings again at Mianchi—truly you lost at sunrise and won before sunset.'

sentences.zh: 方論功賞，以答大勛。』

translations.literal: We are now discussing merit and rewards to answer the great achievement.
translations.idiomatic: We shall weigh merit and grant rewards worthy of this feat.

sentences.zh: 時，赤眉雖降，眾寇猶盛：延岑據藍田，王歆據下邽，芳丹據新豐，蔣震據霸陵，張邯據長安，公孫守據長陵，楊周據谷口，呂鮪據陳倉，角閎據汧，駱延據盩厔，任良據鄠，汝章據槐里，各稱將軍，擁兵多者萬餘，少者數千人，轉相攻擊。

translations.literal: At that time, though the Red Eyebrows had surrendered, the many bandits were still strong: Yan Cen held Lantian, Wang Xin held Xiaqui, Fang Dan held Xinfeng, Jiang Zhen held Baling, Zhang Han held Chang'an, Gongsun Shou held Zhangling, Yang Zhou held Gukou, Lü Wei held Chencang, Jiao Hong held Qian, Luo Yan held Zhouzhi, Ren Liang held Hu, Ru Zhang held Huaili; each called himself general; those who held troops in great number exceeded ten thousand, those in small number several thousand, and they turned on one another in attack.
translations.idiomatic: Even with the Red Eyebrows gone, banditry still swelled: Yan Cen at Lantian, Wang Xin at Xiaqui, Fang Dan at Xinfeng, Jiang Zhen at Baling, Zhang Han in Chang'an, Gongsun Shou at Zhangling, Yang Zhou at Gukou, Lü Wei at Chencang, Jiao Hong at Qian, Luo Yan at Zhouzhi, Ren Liang at Hu, Ru Zhang at Huaili—each styled himself a general, mustering from a few thousand to over ten thousand men and raiding one another endlessly.

sentences.zh: 異且戰且行，屯軍上林苑中。

translations.literal: Yi fought as he marched and encamped his army in the Shanglin Park.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi fought his way forward and pitched camp inside the imperial hunting park at Shanglin.

sentences.zh: 延岑既破赤眉，自稱武安王，拜置牧守，欲據關中，引張邯、任良共攻異。

translations.literal: After Yan Cen had defeated the Red Eyebrows, he styled himself King of Wu'an, appointed shepherds and guardians, wished to occupy the passes and center, and led Zhang Han and Ren Liang jointly to attack Yi.
translations.idiomatic: Once Yan Cen had beaten the Red Eyebrows, he proclaimed himself King of Wu'an, set up his own officials, aimed to hold Guanzhong, and marched Zhang Han and Ren Liang against Feng Yi.

sentences.zh: 異擊破之，斬首千餘級，諸營保守附岑者皆來降歸異。

translations.literal: Yi struck and broke them, beheading more than a thousand; all the garrison camps that had held to Yan Cen came to surrender and attach themselves to Yi.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi routed them, took more than a thousand heads, and every stockade that had backed Yan Cen came over to the Han.

sentences.zh: 岑走攻析，異遣復漢將軍鄧曄、輔漢將軍於匡要擊岑，大破之，隆其將蘇臣等八千餘人。

translations.literal: Cen fled to attack Xi; Yi sent the Restore Han general Deng Ye and the Aid Han general Yu Kuang to intercept and strike Cen, greatly defeated him, and brought over his generals such as Su Chen and others to the number of more than eight thousand men.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Cen struck toward Xi; Feng Yi sent Generals Deng Ye and Yu Kuang to ambush him, broke his army, and induced Su Chen and more than eight thousand of his officers and men to surrender.

sentences.zh: 岑遂自武關走南陽。

translations.literal: Cen thereupon fled from Wu Pass toward Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Cen then bolted through Wu Pass into Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 時，百姓饑餓，人相食，黃金一斤易豆五升。

translations.literal: At that time the common people were starving from hunger; men ate one another; one jin of gold could be exchanged for five sheng of beans.
translations.idiomatic: Famine stalked the land; men fed on one another; a pound of gold bought only five pints of beans.

sentences.zh: 道路斷隔，委輸不至，軍士委以果實為糧。

translations.literal: Roads were cut off and blocked; supply convoys did not arrive; the soldiers relied on fruit for grain.
translations.idiomatic: Supply lines were severed and grain never came; the troops lived on wild fruit.

sentences.zh: 詔拜南陽趙匡為右扶風，將兵助異，並送縑谷，軍中皆稱萬歲。

translations.literal: An edict appointed Zhao Kuang of Nanyang as right overseer of Fufeng, led troops to aid Yi, and together sent silk and grain; in the army all cried 'ten thousand years.'
translations.idiomatic: An edict named Zhao Kuang of Nanyang as right overseer of Fufeng to reinforce Feng Yi with troops, grain, and silk; the camps shouted long life to the throne.

sentences.zh: 異兵食漸盛，乃稍誅擊豪傑不從令者，褒賞降附有功勞者，悉遣其渠帥詣京師，散其眾歸本業。

translations.literal: Yi's troops and food gradually grew abundant; he then gradually executed and struck powerful men who did not obey orders, praised and rewarded those who surrendered and attached themselves and had merit and labor, sent all their chieftains to the capital, and dispersed their hosts to return to their original occupations.
translations.idiomatic: As provisions returned, Feng Yi executed defiant magnates, rewarded loyal defectors, shipped rebel chiefs to Luoyang, and sent the rank and file home to their fields.

sentences.zh: 威行關中，惟呂鮪、張邯、蔣震遣使降蜀，其餘悉平。

translations.literal: His might ran through the passes and center; only Lü Wei, Zhang Han, and Jiang Zhen sent envoys to surrender to Shu; the rest were all pacified.
translations.idiomatic: His authority filled Guanzhong; only Lü Wei, Zhang Han, and Jiang Zhen sent envoys to yield to Gongsun Shu's Shu; every other stronghold submitted.

sentences.zh: 明年，公孫述遣將程焉，將數萬人就呂鮪出屯陳倉。

translations.literal: The next year Gongsun Shu sent his general Cheng Yan with several tens of thousands of men to join Lü Wei and go out to encamp at Chencang.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Gongsun Shu dispatched Cheng Yan with tens of thousands to unite with Lü Wei and fortify Chencang.

sentences.zh: 異與趙匡迎擊，大破之，焉退走漢川。

translations.literal: Yi with Zhao Kuang went to meet and strike them, greatly defeated them, and Yan withdrew fleeing to the Han River region.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi and Zhao Kuang met them in battle, crushed them, and drove Cheng Yan back toward the Han River basin.

sentences.zh: 異追戰於箕谷，復破之，還擊破呂鮪，營保降者甚眾。

translations.literal: Yi pursued and fought at Ji Valley, again defeated them, returned and struck and broke Lü Wei, and those who surrendered from the garrison camps were very many.
translations.idiomatic: He pursued them into Ji Valley, broke them again, then swung back to shatter Lü Wei, bringing hosts of garrisons to capitulate.

sentences.zh: 其後蜀復數遣將間出，異輒摧挫之。

translations.literal: After that Shu again several times sent generals out by secret routes; Yi always crushed and broke them.
translations.idiomatic: Shu kept slipping raiding columns through the lines; Feng Yi smashed each one.

sentences.zh: 懷來百姓，申理枉結，出入三歲，上林成都。

translations.literal: He cherished and drew the common people, extended justice to unjust bonds; going out and in three years, Shanglin became like Chengdu.
translations.idiomatic: He won the people by kindness, cleared old wrongs, and within three years the waste of Shanglin bloomed again like Chengdu.

sentences.zh: 異自以久在外，不自安，上書思慕闕廷，願親帷幄，帝不許。

translations.literal: Yi himself thought he had long been outside and was not at ease; he submitted a memorial longing for the palace court and wishing to attend personally within the curtain; the emperor did not permit it.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi, uneasy after so long in the field, begged to return to court and serve at the emperor's side; the throne refused.

sentences.zh: 後人有章言異專制關中，斬長安令，威權至重，百姓歸心，號為『咸陽王』。

translations.literal: Later someone submitted a memorial accusing Yi of acting arbitrarily in the passes and center, executing the magistrate of Chang'an, wielding authority to the utmost weight, so that the hundred surnames turned their hearts to him and called him King of Xianyang.
translations.idiomatic: Later a memorial charged him with ruling Guanzhong on his own authority, executing the magistrate of Chang'an, and holding such awe that the people hailed him as king of Xianyang.

sentences.zh: 帝使以章示異。

translations.literal: The emperor had the memorial shown to Yi.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sent him the accusation to read.

sentences.zh: 異惶懼，上書謝曰：『臣本諸生，遭遇受命之會，充備行伍，過蒙恩私，位大將，爵通侯，受任方面，以立微功，皆自國家謀慮，愚臣無所能及。

translations.literal: Yi was frightened and fearful; he submitted a memorial apologizing, saying: 'Your servant was originally a student; encountering the meeting of receiving the mandate, I filled a place in the ranks, excessively received private favor, rank of great general, noble rank of full marquis, received appointment in my quarter to establish slight merit—all from the state's planning and deliberation; your foolish servant could not reach it.
translations.idiomatic: Trembling, Feng Yi wrote: 'I was only a scholar when fate raised this house; I rose through the ranks, received honors beyond desert—grand general, full marquis, command of a region—and won meager merit only because the state mapped every step; no wit of mine could match that.'

sentences.zh: 臣伏自思惟：以詔敕戰攻，每輒如意；

translations.literal: Your servant has bowed and reflected: when by edict and order we fought and attacked, each time it went as wished.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever I fought by your sealed orders, fortune favored us.

sentences.zh: 時以私心斷決，未嘗不有悔。

translations.literal: When at times I decided by private heart, never was there not regret.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever I followed my own judgment, I lived to regret it.

sentences.zh: 國家獨見之明，久而益遠，乃知「性與天道，不可得而聞也」。

translations.literal: The state's sole-seeing clarity, long enduring, grows ever more far-reaching; then I knew that "nature and the way of Heaven cannot be got by hearing."
translations.idiomatic: Your vision alone, steady and deepening, taught me what Confucius meant: "Nature and Heaven's way cannot be heard from a teacher."'

sentences.zh: 當兵革始起，擾攘之時，豪傑競逐，迷惑千數。

translations.literal: When weapons and armor first rose and it was a time of turmoil, heroes competed in pursuit, confused in their thousands.
translations.idiomatic: In the first chaos of war, rival chiefs swarmed by the thousand, each chasing glory.'

sentences.zh: 臣以遭遇，托身聖明，在傾危混淆之中，尚不敢過差，而況天下平定，上尊下卑，而臣爵位所蒙，巍巍不測乎？

translations.literal: Your servant by encountering fate entrusted his person to sagely brightness; in the midst of leaning peril and confusion still did not dare exceed error—how much more now that under heaven is pacified, above honored and below humble, and the noble rank and position your servant has received is lofty and unfathomable?
translations.idiomatic: I clung to your light amid collapse and murk and still dared not stumble—now that the realm is ordered, high and low know their place, and my rank towers beyond measure, how could I?'

sentences.zh: 誠冀以謹敕，遂自終始。

translations.literal: Truly I hope by careful discipline to reach from beginning to end.
translations.idiomatic: I pray only to govern myself with care from first to last.'

sentences.zh: 見所示臣章，戰栗怖懼。

translations.literal: Seeing the memorial shown your servant, I tremble with fear.
translations.idiomatic: Reading the charge laid against me, I shake with dread.'

sentences.zh: 伏念明主知臣愚性，固敢因緣自陳。

translations.literal: Bowing I reflect that the enlightened lord knows your servant's foolish nature; therefore I dare take the occasion to state myself.
translations.idiomatic: Yet I trust you know my dull honesty; I venture this plea all the same.'

sentences.zh: 』詔報曰：『將軍之於國家，義為君臣，恩猶父子。

translations.literal: ' The edict replied, saying: 'Between the general and the state, in righteousness you are ruler and minister; in kindness you are still like father and son.
translations.idiomatic: The rescript answered: 'Between you and the throne the bond is minister and sovereign, yet the kindness is like father and son.'

sentences.zh: 何嫌何疑，而有懼意？』

translations.literal: What suspicion, what doubt, that you should have a heart of fear?'
translations.idiomatic: What shadow of distrust should make you afraid?'

sentences.zh: 六年春，異朝京師。

translations.literal: In the spring of the sixth year Yi had audience at the capital.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year of Jianwu Feng Yi came to court at Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 引見，帝謂公卿曰：『是我起兵時主簿也。

translations.literal: He was led in to audience; the emperor said to the dukes and ministers: 'This is the chief clerk from when I first raised troops.
translations.idiomatic: During the audience he told the high ministers, 'Here is the chief clerk who stood with me when I first took arms.'

sentences.zh: 為吾披荊棘，定關中。

translations.literal: For me he cleared brambles and thorns and settled the passes and center.
translations.idiomatic: He cut a path through the brambles and brought peace to Guanzhong.'

sentences.zh: 』既罷，使中黃門賜以珍寶、衣服、錢、帛。

translations.literal: ' When the audience ended, he had a palace eunuch of the yellow gate bestow precious objects, clothes, cash, and silk.
translations.idiomatic: After court he sent a palace eunuch with gems, robes, cash, and silk.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『倉卒無蔞亭豆粥，虖沱河麥飯，厚意久不報。

translations.literal: The edict said: 'In haste at Wulou Pavilion there was the bean gruel; at the Hutuo River there was the wheat rice—the deep kindness has long gone unanswered.
translations.idiomatic: His message read: 'The porridge at Wulou, the millet at the Hutuo crossing—your kindness went long unrepaid.'

sentences.zh: 』異稽首謝曰：『臣聞管仲謂桓公曰：「願君無忘射鉤，臣無忘檻車。

translations.literal: ' Yi knocked his head in thanks and said: 'Your servant has heard that Guan Zhong said to Duke Huan: "I hope the lord will not forget the arrow in the hook; your servant will not forget the caged cart.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi kowtowed and said, 'Guan Zhong told Duke Huan, "May my lord remember the arrow that nearly killed you; may I remember the prison cart that spared me."'

sentences.zh: 」齊國賴之。

translations.literal: " Qi state relied on this.
translations.idiomatic: On that covenant Qi built its greatness.'

sentences.zh: 臣今亦願國家無忘河北之難，小臣不敢忘巾車之恩。

translations.literal: Your servant now also wishes the state not to forget the hardship north of the Yellow River; the small servant dares not forget the kindness at Jinche.
translations.idiomatic: So I pray the court will not forget the trials in Hebei, nor I the meal you shared at Jinche.'

sentences.zh: 』後數引宴見，定議圖蜀，留十餘日，令異妻子隨異還西。

translations.literal: ' Later he several times led him in feasts and audiences, settled plans to map Shu, detained him more than ten days, and ordered Yi's wife and children to follow Yi back west.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward the emperor feasted him often, fixed the strategy against Shu, kept him ten days, then sent him west again with his family.

sentences.zh: 夏，遣諸將上隴，為隗囂所敗，乃詔異軍栒邑。

translations.literal: In summer he sent the various generals up Long and they were defeated by Wei Ao; he thereupon ordered Yi's army to Xunyi.
translations.idiomatic: That summer columns sent onto Long were beaten by Wei Ao; the emperor shifted Feng Yi's command to Xunyi.

sentences.zh: 未及至，隗囂乘勝使其將王元、行巡將二萬餘人下隴，因分遣巡取栒邑。

translations.literal: Before he arrived, Wei Ao, riding victory, sent his generals Wang Yuan and Xing Xun with more than twenty thousand men down Long, and thereupon separately sent Xun to seize Xunyi.
translations.idiomatic: Before Feng Yi could arrive, Wei Ao exploited his win to push Wang Yuan and Xing Xun with over twenty thousand men off the Long plateau, detaching Xing Xun to snap up Xunyi.

sentences.zh: 異即馳兵，欲先據之。

translations.literal: Yi immediately galloped his troops, wishing to seize it first.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi spurred his army to reach the town first.

sentences.zh: 諸將皆曰：『虜兵盛而新乘勝，不可與爭，宜止軍便地，徐思方略。

translations.literal: The generals all said: 'The enemy troops are strong and newly ride victory; we cannot contend with them; it is fitting to halt the army in a convenient place and slowly think strategy.
translations.idiomatic: His officers urged: 'The foe is fresh from victory and too strong to meet; halt on favorable ground and plan at leisure.'

sentences.zh: 』異曰：『虜兵臨境，忸忕小利，遂欲深入。

translations.literal: ' Yi said: 'The enemy troops press the border, fix on petty gain, and then wish to penetrate deeply.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi replied: 'They covet a quick prize on the frontier and mean to drive deep.'

sentences.zh: 若得栒邑，三輔動搖，是吾憂也。

translations.literal: If they get Xunyi, the three approaches will waver; this is my worry.
translations.idiomatic: If they take Xunyi, the capital region reels—that is what I fear.'

sentences.zh: 夫「攻者不足，守者有餘」。

translations.literal: The saying runs, "The attacker lacks enough; the defender has surplus."
translations.idiomatic: The old maxim says, "Attackers run short; defenders hold the edge."'

sentences.zh: 今先據城，以逸待勞，非所以爭也。

translations.literal: Now first to seize the city, with ease awaiting toil, is not what is called contending.
translations.idiomatic: Seize the walls first, rest while they tire—that is not reckless battle.'

sentences.zh: 』潛往閉城，偃旗鼓。

translations.literal: ' He went secretly, closed the city, and laid drums and banners flat.
translations.idiomatic: He slipped in, barred the gates, and hid drums and flags.

sentences.zh: 行巡不足，馳赴之。

translations.literal: Xing Xun was unprepared and galloped to reach it.
translations.idiomatic: Xing Xun, caught off guard, raced to claim the place.

sentences.zh: 異乘其不意。

translations.literal: Yi took advantage of his not expecting it.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi struck while he was unwary.

sentences.zh: 卒擊鼓建旗而出。

translations.literal: Suddenly they struck drums and raised banners and went out.
translations.idiomatic: Suddenly the Han beat drums, raised standards, and sortied.

sentences.zh: 巡軍驚亂奔走，追擊數十里，大破之。

translations.literal: Xun's army was alarmed and chaotic in flight; they pursued several tens of li and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Xing Xun's men panicked and fled; Feng Yi chased them tens of li and broke them utterly.

sentences.zh: 祭遵亦破王元於汧。

translations.literal: Zhai Zun also defeated Wang Yuan at Qian.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Zun likewise crushed Wang Yuan at Qian.

sentences.zh: 於是北地諸豪長耿定等，悉畔隗囂降。

translations.literal: Thereupon the northern land magnates such as Geng Ding and others all turned from Wei Ao and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Then the great clans of Beidi, led by Geng Ding, abandoned Wei Ao for the Han.

sentences.zh: 異上書言狀，不敢自伐。

translations.literal: Yi submitted a memorial stating the situation and did not dare boast of himself.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi reported the victory without praising himself.

sentences.zh: 諸將或欲分其功，帝患之。

translations.literal: Some of the generals wished to divide his merit; the emperor was troubled by it.
translations.idiomatic: Other generals tried to steal credit; the emperor found it distasteful.

sentences.zh: 乃下璽書曰：『制詔大司馬，虎牙、建威、漢忠、捕虜、武威將軍：虜兵猥下，三輔驚恐。

translations.literal: He thereupon issued an edict with the seal, saying: 'Imperial rescript to the grand marshal, the Tiger Fang, Jianwei, Hanzhong, Capture Barbarian, and Wuwei generals: enemy troops have swarmed down; the three approaches are frightened and alarmed.
translations.idiomatic: He issued a sealed edict to the grand marshal and the Tiger Fang, Jianwei, Hanzhong, Capture Barbarian, and Wuwei generals: 'The enemy has poured down Long; the three approaches tremble.'

sentences.zh: 栒邑危亡，在於旦夕。

translations.literal: Xunyi is in peril of perishing between morning and evening.
translations.idiomatic: Xunyi may fall by nightfall.'

sentences.zh: 北地營保，按兵觀望。

translations.literal: The northern land garrison camps hold their troops and watch.
translations.idiomatic: The Beidi stockades sit idle, watching which way the wind blows.'

sentences.zh: 今偏城獲全，虜兵挫折，使耿定之屬，復念君臣之義。

translations.literal: Now the isolated city has been preserved whole, enemy troops have been checked and broken, and those like Geng Ding have again remembered the righteousness of ruler and minister.
translations.idiomatic: Because this lone city stands and the invader is bloodied, men like Geng Ding remember their duty to the throne.'

sentences.zh: 征西功若丘山，猶自以為不足。

translations.literal: The western expedition's merit is like hills and mountains, yet he still thinks it not enough.
translations.idiomatic: The western commander's merit towers like a mountain, yet he calls it insufficient.'

sentences.zh: 孟之反奔而殿，亦何異哉？

translations.literal: Meng Zhifan ran but covered the rear—how is this different?'
translations.idiomatic: Is he not another Meng Zhifan, who ran yet shielded the army?'

sentences.zh: 今遣太中大夫賜征西吏士死傷者醫藥、棺斂，大司馬已下親吊死問疾，以崇謙讓。

translations.literal: Now we send a grand counsellor of the palace to bestow on the western expedition's officers and soldiers who died or were wounded medicine and coffin burial; from the grand marshal downward they shall personally mourn the dead and inquire after the sick, to honor modest yielding.
translations.idiomatic: We dispatch a palace grandee with medicines and coffins for the wounded and the dead, and order every commander down to the grand marshal to mourn the fallen and visit the sick, honoring humility.'

sentences.zh: 』於是使異進軍義渠，並領北地太守事。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon he had Yi advance the army to Yiqu and concurrently hold the duties of governor of Beidi.
translations.idiomatic: He then ordered Feng Yi toward Yiqu and added the governorship of Beidi.

sentences.zh: 青山胡率萬餘人降異。

translations.literal: The Qing Mountain Hu led more than ten thousand men to surrender to Yi.
translations.idiomatic: The Qing Mountain Xiongnu brought over ten thousand warriors to submit.

sentences.zh: 異又擊盧芳將賈覽、匈奴薁日逐王，破之。

translations.literal: Yi again attacked Lu Fang's general Jia Lan and the Xiongnu Worthy King Wurizhu, and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: He next defeated Lu Fang's general Jia Lan and the Xiongnu Worthy King Wurizhu.

sentences.zh: 上郡、安定皆降，異復領安定太守事。

translations.literal: Shang commandery and Anding both surrendered; Yi again held the duties of governor of Anding.
translations.idiomatic: Shang and Anding yielded; Feng Yi was given Anding as well.

sentences.zh: 九年春，祭遵卒，詔異守征虜將軍，並將其營。

translations.literal: In the spring of the ninth year Zhun Zun died; an edict ordered Yi to act as general who captures barbarians and to merge command of his camp.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth year Zhai Zun died; the court named Feng Yi acting general who captures barbarians and merged their camps.

sentences.zh: 及隗囂死，其將王元、周宗等復立囂子純，猶總兵據冀，公孫述遣將趙匡等救之，帝復令異行天水太守事。

translations.literal: When Wei Ao died, his generals Wang Yuan, Zhou Zong, and others again set up Ao's son Chun, still gathering troops and holding Ji; Gongsun Shu sent his general Zhao Kuang and others to rescue them; the emperor again ordered Yi to act as governor of Tianshui.
translations.idiomatic: After Wei Ao's death Wang Yuan and Zhou Zong raised Ao's son Wei Chun at Ji; Gongsun Shu sent Zhao Kuang to relieve them, and the emperor again put Tianshui under Feng Yi's charge.

sentences.zh: 攻匡等且一年，皆斬之。

translations.literal: He attacked Kuang and the others for nearly a year and beheaded them all.
translations.idiomatic: He besieged Zhao Kuang's ring for nearly a year and executed them all.

sentences.zh: 諸將共攻冀，不能拔，欲且還休兵，異固持不動，常為眾軍鋒。

translations.literal: The generals jointly attacked Ji but could not take it; they wished for the moment to withdraw and rest the troops; Yi firmly held unmoved and always was the spearhead of the mass of armies.
translations.idiomatic: The allied generals could not storm Ji and wanted to pull back; Feng Yi held his ground and always led the van.

sentences.zh: 明年夏，與諸將攻落門，未拔，病發，薨於軍，謚曰節侯。

translations.literal: The next summer he with the various generals attacked Luomen; before it was taken illness broke out; he died in the army; posthumous title Marquis of Integrity.
translations.idiomatic: The next summer he joined the assault on Luomen; the city had not fallen when he fell ill and died in camp, posthumously honored as Marquis Jie.

sentences.zh: 長子彰嗣。

translations.literal: The eldest son Zhang inherited.
translations.idiomatic: His eldest son Feng Zhang succeeded to the title.

sentences.zh: 明年，帝思異功，復封彰弟訢為析鄉侯。

translations.literal: The next year the emperor thought of Yi's merit and again enfeoffed Zhang's younger brother Xin as marquis of Xixiang.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, remembering Feng Yi's service, the court also enfeoffed his brother Feng Xin as marquis of Xixiang.

sentences.zh: 十三年，更封彰東緡侯，食三縣。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year Zhang's fief was changed to marquis of Dongmin, with food income from three counties.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirteenth year Feng Zhang was moved to the marquisate of Dongmin with revenue from three counties.

sentences.zh: 永平中，徙封平鄉侯。

translations.literal: In the Yongping period his fief was moved to marquis of Pingxiang.
translations.idiomatic: Under Yongping his seat was shifted to the marquisate of Pingxiang.

sentences.zh: 彰卒，子普嗣，有罪，國除。

translations.literal: Zhang died; his son Pu inherited; there was a crime and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Zhang died and his son Pu inherited, but a crime stripped the house of its fief.

sentences.zh: 永初六年，安帝下詔曰：『夫仁不遺親，義不忘勞，興滅繼絕，善善及子孫，古之典也。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of Yongchu the Emperor An issued an edict, saying: 'Benevolence does not cast off kin; righteousness does not forget labor; raising the fallen and continuing the cut off, doing good reaches to sons and grandsons—these are the canons of antiquity.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 6 Emperor An proclaimed: 'Humaneness cherishes kin, righteousness remembers toil, fallen houses must be raised, and merit should bless descendants—such was the ancient rule.'

sentences.zh: 昔我光武受命中興，恢弘聖緒，橫被四表，昭假上下，光耀萬世，祉祚流衍，垂於罔極。

translations.literal: In antiquity our Guangwu received the mandate and restored the rise, enlarged the sage line, spread across the four outer regions, shone bright above and below, illuminated ten thousand generations; blessings and fortune flowed and extended, hanging to limitless ends.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu received Heaven's charge and revived the Han, he widened the royal work until its glory filled the four seas and lit the ages; his blessing still runs without end.'

sentences.zh: 予末小子，夙夜永思，追惟勛烈，披圖案籍，建武元功二十八將，佐命虎臣，讖記有征。

translations.literal: I, the least boy, morning and night ever think, trace and consider meritorious deeds, spread charts and examine registers—the twenty-eight generals of first merit at Jianwu, tiger ministers who aided the mandate, the prognostications record their signs.
translations.idiomatic: I, immature as I am, pore night and day over the charts: the twenty-eight champions of Jianwu, the tiger ministers who bore the mandate, stand written in the portents.'

sentences.zh: 蓋蕭、曹紹封，傳繼於今；

translations.literal: As for Xiao and Cao continuing enfeoffment, transmission continues to the present;
translations.idiomatic: Xiao He and Cao Shen kept their lines unbroken to this day;'

sentences.zh: 況此未遠，而或至乏祀，朕其湣之。

translations.literal: how much more these are not far, yet some have reached exhaustion of sacrifice; I pity them.
translations.idiomatic: these heroes are closer in time, yet some lack heirs—I grieve for them.'

sentences.zh: 其條二十八將無嗣絕世，若犯罪奪國，其子孫應當統後者，分別署狀上。

translations.literal: Let there be drawn up a list of the twenty-eight generals without heirs, cut off from the world, or if by crime the state was taken, of sons and grandsons who ought to carry on the line, separately set down in memorials upward.
translations.idiomatic: List every one of the twenty-eight whose line died out or whose fief was lost to crime, and note any descendant fit to carry the title, each case on its own memorial.'

sentences.zh: 將及景風，章敘舊德，顯茲遺功焉。

translations.literal: We shall reach the Jing wind; let documents set forth old virtue and display these remaining achievements.
translations.idiomatic: When the Jing-wind rites come round, we shall publish their old virtue and honor these unfinished deeds.'

sentences.zh: 』於是紹封普子晨為平鄉侯。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon he continued the enfeoffment and made Pu's son Chen marquis of Pingxiang.
translations.idiomatic: He therefore restored the line, naming Pu's son Chen marquis of Pingxiang.

sentences.zh: 明年，二十八將絕國者，皆紹封焉。

translations.literal: The next year, those of the twenty-eight generals whose states had been cut off were all continued in enfeoffment.
translations.idiomatic: The next year every extinguished house among the twenty-eight was re-enfeoffed.

sentences.zh: 岑彭字君然，南陽棘陽人也。

translations.literal: Cen Peng, courtesy name Junran, was a man of Jiyang in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Cen Peng, styled Junran, came from Jiyang in Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 王莽時，守本縣長。

translations.literal: In the time of Wang Mang he guarded his native county as magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: Under Wang Mang he served as magistrate of his home county.

sentences.zh: 漢兵起，攻拔棘陽，彭將家屬奔前隊大夫甄阜。

translations.literal: When the Han troops rose, they attacked and took Jiyang; Peng led his household dependents and fled to the forward army grandee Zhen Fu.
translations.idiomatic: When the Han armies rose and seized Jiyang, Cen Peng led his family to the forward-army grandee Zhen Fu.

sentences.zh: 阜怒彭不能固守，拘彭母妻，令效功自襯。

translations.literal: Fu was angry that Peng could not hold firm in defense; he detained Peng's mother and wife and ordered him to prove merit so as to redeem himself.
translations.idiomatic: Zhen Fu, furious that Cen Peng had not held the town, held his mother and wife hostage and ordered him to earn his way back with battlefield merit.

sentences.zh: 彭將賓客戰鬥甚力。

translations.literal: Peng led his retainers in battle and fought very fiercely.
translations.idiomatic: Cen Peng led his clients into battle and fought with exceptional stubbornness.

sentences.zh: 及甄阜死，彭被創，亡歸宛，與前隊貳嚴說共城守。

translations.literal: When Zhen Fu died, Peng was wounded and fled to Wan; together with the forward army deputy Yan Shuo he jointly defended the city walls.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhen Fu fell, Cen Peng was wounded and fled to Wan, where he and Yan Shuo, deputy of the forward army, held the walls together.

sentences.zh: 漢兵攻之數月，城中糧盡，人相食，彭乃與說舉城降。

translations.literal: The Han troops attacked them several months; grain in the city was exhausted and men ate one another; Peng thereupon with Shuo raised the city and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: The Han besieged them for months until the granaries were empty and cannibalism began; Cen Peng and Yan Shuo then surrendered the whole city.

sentences.zh: 諸將欲誅之，大司徒伯升曰：『彭，郡之大吏，執心堅守，是其節也。

translations.literal: The generals wished to execute them; the grand minister over the masses Bosheng said: 'Peng is a great officer of the commandery; holding his heart firm in defense is his integrity.
translations.idiomatic: The generals wanted them beheaded, but Grand Minister Liu Bosheng said, 'Cen Peng was a senior official who defended his post to the end—that is constancy.'

sentences.zh: 今舉大事，當表義士，不如封之，以勸其後。

translations.literal: Now in raising the great enterprise we should display men of righteousness; it is better to enfeoff him so as to encourage those who come after.
translations.idiomatic: As we launch this great cause we ought to honor such loyalty; enfeoff him and others will take heart.'

sentences.zh: 』更始乃封彭為歸德侯，令屬伯升。

translations.literal: ' Gengshi thereupon enfeoffed Peng as marquis of Guide and ordered him to belong to Bosheng.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi then named Cen Peng marquis of Guide and attached him to Bosheng's command.'

sentences.zh: 及伯升遇害，彭復為大司馬朱鮪校尉，從鮪擊王莽楊州牧李聖，殺之，定淮陽城。

translations.literal: When Bosheng met harm, Peng again became a colonel under grand marshal Zhu You, followed You in attacking Wang Mang's Yangzhou shepherd Li Sheng, killed him, and pacified Huaiyang city.
translations.idiomatic: After Bosheng was murdered, Cen Peng again served as colonel under Grand Marshal Zhu You, joined him against Wang Mang's Yangzhou governor Li Sheng, slew Li, and secured Huaiyang.

sentences.zh: 鮪薦彭為淮陽都尉。

translations.literal: You recommended Peng as commandant of Huaiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu You recommended him for the Huaiyang commandancy.

sentences.zh: 更始遣立威王張卬與將軍徭偉鎮淮陽。

translations.literal: Gengshi sent the king who establishes might Zhang Ang and the general Yao Wei to garrison Huaiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi dispatched the Prince who Establishes Might Zhang Ang and General Yao Wei to hold Huaiyang.

sentences.zh: 偉反，擊走卬。

translations.literal: Wei rebelled, struck and drove off Ang.
translations.idiomatic: Yao Wei mutinied, routed Zhang Ang, and drove him out.

sentences.zh: 彭引兵攻偉，破之。

translations.literal: Peng led troops to attack Wei and defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Cen Peng marched against Yao Wei and broke his force.

sentences.zh: 遷潁川太守。

translations.literal: He was promoted governor of Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: He was transferred to governor of Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 會舂陵劉茂起兵，略下潁川，彭不得之官，乃與麾下數百人從河內太守邑人韓歆。

translations.literal: It happened that Liu Mao of Chunling raised troops and raided Yingchuan; Peng could not reach his post and therefore with several hundred men under his command followed his townsman Han Xin, governor of Henei.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Mao of Chunling rebelled and overran Yingchuan, Cen Peng could not take up office and instead followed several hundred followers to his fellow townsman Han Xin, the governor of Henei.

sentences.zh: 會光武徇河內，歆議欲城守，彭止不聽。

translations.literal: It happened that Guangwu campaigned through Henei; Xin deliberated and wished to defend the city; Peng stopped him and he did not listen.
translations.idiomatic: As Guangwu advanced through Henei, Han Xin debated holding the city; Cen Peng dissuaded him, but Han Xin would not listen.

sentences.zh: 既而光武至懷，歆迫急迎降。

translations.literal: Afterward Guangwu reached Huai; Xin, pressed tight, came out in welcome and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu reached Huai, Han Xin—boxed in—opened the gates and submitted.

sentences.zh: 光武知其謀，大怒，收歆置鼓下，將斬之。

translations.literal: Guangwu knew his plot and was greatly angry; he seized Xin and placed him beneath the drum, about to behead him.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu, learning of the earlier plot, was furious: he had Han Xin bound beneath the army drum and prepared to execute him.

sentences.zh: 召見彭，彭因進說曰：『今赤眉入關，更始危殆，權臣放縱，矯稱詔制，道路阻塞，四方蜂起，群雄競逐，百姓無所歸命。

translations.literal: He summoned Peng for audience; Peng thereupon advanced persuasion, saying: 'Now the Red Eyebrows have entered the passes; Gengshi is in peril and danger; powerful ministers give free rein, falsely claiming edicts and orders; roads are blocked and cut; the four quarters swarm like wasps; heroes compete in pursuit; the hundred surnames have nowhere to entrust their lives.
translations.idiomatic: Called before Guangwu, Cen Peng urged: 'The Red Eyebrows are in Guanzhong, Gengshi totters, favorites forge orders, roads are cut, rebels rise on every side, and the people have no master.'

sentences.zh: 竊聞大王平河北，開王業，此誠皇天祐漢，士人之福也。

translations.literal: I have heard in secret that the great king has pacified north of the Yellow River and opened kingly enterprise—this is truly high Heaven aiding Han and the fortune of gentlemen.
translations.idiomatic: They say you have pacified Hebei and laid the foundations of a new dynasty—Heaven's favor to Han and a blessing to every loyal man.'

sentences.zh: 彭幸蒙司徒公所見全濟，未有報德，旋被禍難，永恨於心。

translations.literal: Peng was fortunate to receive the grand minister over the masses' seeing and complete rescue; I had not yet repaid virtue when suddenly I met calamity and disaster; eternal regret remained in the heart.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Minister Bosheng once spared my life; I never repaid him before disaster struck, and that grief has never left me.

sentences.zh: 今復遭遇，願出身自效。

translations.literal: Now again encountering this, I wish to offer my person to prove effect.
translations.idiomatic: Now fate sets me before you again; I beg to serve with my whole strength.'

sentences.zh: 』光武深接納之。

translations.literal: ' Guangwu deeply received and accepted him.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu welcomed him warmly and took him in.'

sentences.zh: 彭因言韓歆南陽大人，可以為用。

translations.literal: Peng thereupon said Han Xin of Nanyang was a great man of the region and could be employed.
translations.idiomatic: Cen Peng added that Han Xin was a leading figure of Nanyang who could still be useful.

sentences.zh: 乃貰歆，以為鄧禹軍師。

translations.literal: Thereupon he pardoned Xin and made him military adviser to Deng Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu spared Han Xin and named him Deng Yu's army adviser.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，使彭擊荊州，下犨、葉等十餘城。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu he sent Peng to strike Jingzhou; he took Chou, Ye, and more than ten cities.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 the emperor sent Cen Peng against Jingzhou; he captured Chou, Ye, and over ten towns.

sentences.zh: 是時，南方尤亂。

translations.literal: At that time the south was especially in disorder.
translations.idiomatic: The south was in utter chaos.

sentences.zh: 南郡人秦豐據黎丘，自稱楚黎王，略十有二縣；

translations.literal: A man of Nan commandery, Qin Feng, held Liqiu, styled himself King Chu Li of Li, and seized twelve counties;
translations.idiomatic: Qin Feng of Nan commandery held Liqiu, called himself King Chu-Li, and overran twelve counties;

sentences.zh: 董訢起堵鄉；

translations.literal: Dong Xin rose at Duxiang.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Xin raised a revolt at Duxiang.

sentences.zh: 許邯起杏；

translations.literal: Xu Han rose at Xing.
translations.idiomatic: Xu Han seized Xing and set up his own camp.

sentences.zh: 又，更始諸將各擁兵據南陽諸城。

translations.literal: moreover the various generals of Gengshi each held troops and occupied the cities of Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile Gengshi's generals still garrisoned the Nanyang towns.

sentences.zh: 帝遣吳漢伐之，漢軍所過多侵暴。

translations.literal: The emperor sent Wu Han to punish them; wherever Han's army passed it mostly encroached and was violent.
translations.idiomatic: He dispatched Wu Han, whose columns plundered and abused the countryside as they marched.

sentences.zh: 時，破虜將軍鄧奉謁歸新野，怒吳漢掠其鄉里，遂反，擊破漢軍，獲其輜重，屯據淯陽，與諸賊合從。

translations.literal: At that time the general who breaks the barbarians Deng Feng, on leave visiting Xinye, was angry that Wu Han plundered his village and district; thereupon he rebelled, struck and broke Han's army, captured his baggage train, encamped and held Yuyang, and joined the various bandits in vertical alliance.
translations.idiomatic: General Deng Feng was home in Xinye on leave when Wu Han ravaged his district; he revolted, shattered Wu Han's corps, seized the baggage, fortified Yuyang, and allied with the other rebels.

sentences.zh: 秋，彭破杏，降許邯，遷征南大將軍。

translations.literal: In autumn Peng broke Xing, subdued Xu Han, and was promoted grand general on the south.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn Cen Peng took Xing, forced Xu Han to yield, and was promoted grand general of the southern expedition.

sentences.zh: 復遣朱祐、賈復及建威大將軍耿弇，漢忠將軍王常，武威將軍郭守，越騎將軍劉宏，偏將軍劉嘉、耿植等，與彭並力討鄧奉。

translations.literal: Again he sent Zhu You, Jia Fu, and grand general who establishes might Geng Yan, Hanzhong general Wang Chang, Wuwei general Guo Shou, chief of yue cavalry Liu Hong, lieutenant generals Liu Jia and Geng Zhi and others, with Peng joining strength to punish Deng Feng.
translations.idiomatic: He reinforced Cen Peng with Zhu You, Jia Fu, Geng Yan, Wang Chang, Guo Shou, Liu Hong, Liu Jia, Geng Zhi, and others for the campaign against Deng Feng.

sentences.zh: 先擊堵鄉，而奉將萬餘人救董訢。

translations.literal: First they struck Duxiang, while Feng led more than ten thousand men to rescue Dong Xin.
translations.idiomatic: They hit Duxiang first; Deng Feng brought ten thousand men to relieve Dong Xin.

sentences.zh: 訢、奉皆南陽精兵，彭等攻之，連月不克。

translations.literal: Xin and Feng were both crack troops of Nanyang; Peng and the others attacked them and for successive months could not overcome them.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Xin and Deng Feng commanded Nanyang's best soldiers, and for months the imperial army could not break them.

sentences.zh: 三年夏，帝自將南征，至葉，董訁斤別將將數千人遮首，車騎不可得前。

translations.literal: In the summer of the third year the emperor personally led a southern expedition; when he reached Ye, a lieutenant general of Dong Xin led several thousand men to block the road ahead so that chariots and cavalry could not advance.
translations.idiomatic: In the third summer the emperor marched south to Ye, where one of Dong Xin's officers barred the way with several thousand men and stopped the chariots and horse.

sentences.zh: 彭奔擊，大破之。

translations.literal: Peng galloped to attack and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Cen Peng charged them and scattered their line.

sentences.zh: 帝至堵陽，鄧奉夜逃歸淯陽，董訁斤降。

translations.literal: The emperor reached Duyang; Deng Feng fled by night back to Yuyang; Dong Xin surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: At Duyang Deng Feng fled by night to Yuyang while Dong Xin capitulated.

sentences.zh: 彭復與耿弇、賈復及積弩將軍傅俊、騎都尉臧宮等從追鄧奉於小長安，帝率諸將親戰，大破之。

translations.literal: Peng again with Geng Yan, Jia Fu, and general of accumulated crossbows Fu Jun, chief of cavalry Zang Gong and others followed in pursuing Deng Feng at Xiao Chang'an; the emperor led the generals in person in battle and greatly defeated them.
